{"file_path": "/home/leul/Documents/task-7/repo/studioops/API_tests/test_booking_cancellation_window.py", "content": """"
HTTP-layer tests for the 12-hour cancellation/reschedule window.

Within 12 hours: operations are ALLOWED but flagged as breaches and a
late-cancel credit penalty is applied.
Outside 12 hours: operations succeed with no breach.
"""
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

from app.models.studio import StudioSession, Reservation
from app.models.analytics import CreditHistory
from app.services.booking_service import create_reservation


def _login(client, username="testcustomer", password="TestPass123!"):
    return client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        data={"identifier": username, "password": password},
        follow_redirects=True,
    )


# ── Fixtures ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

@pytest.fixture
def imminent_session(db, sample_users, sample_room):
    """A session starting in 6 hours (inside the 12-hour breach window)."""
    start = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(hours=6)
    s = StudioSession(
        title="Imminent Yoga",
        instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id,
        room_id=sample_room.id,
        start_time=start,
        end_time=start + timedelta(hours=1),
        capacity=15,
        is_active=True,
    )
    db.session.add(s)
    db.session.commit()
    return s


@pytest.fixture
def imminent_reservation(db, sample_users, imminent_session):
    """A confirmed reservation on the imminent session."""
    result = create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, imminent_session.id)
    return Reservation.query.get(result["reservation_id"])


@pytest.fixture
def future_session(db, sample_users, sample_room):
    """A session starting in 48 hours (safely outside the 12-hour window)."""
    start = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(hours=48)
    s = StudioSession(
        title="Future Yoga",
        instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id,
        room_id=sample_room.id,
        start_time=start,
        end_time=start + timedelta(hours=1),
        capacity=15,
        is_active=True,
    )
    db.session.add(s)
    db.session.commit()
    return s


# ── Cancel within 12 hours — allowed, breach recorded ─────────────────────────

class TestCancelWithin12Hours:
    def test_cancel_returns_200(self, client, sample_users, imminent_reservation):
        """POST /booking/<id>/cancel within 12 h is allowed and returns 200."""
        _login(client)
        resp = client.post(f"/booking/{imminent_reservation.id}/cancel")
        assert resp.status_code == 200

    def test_cancel_sets_reservation_canceled(self, client, db, sample_users, imminent_reservation):
        """Reservation status becomes 'canceled' after a late cancel."""
        _login(client)
        client.post(f"/booking/{imminent_reservation.id}/cancel")
        db.session.refresh(imminent_reservation)
        assert imminent_reservation.status == "canceled"

    def test_cancel_sets_breach_flag(self, client, db, sample_users, imminent_reservation):
        """breach_flag is True after a late cancel."""
        _login(client)
        client.post(f"/booking/{imminent_reservation.id}/cancel")
        db.session.refresh(imminent_reservation)
        assert imminent_reservation.breach_flag is True

    def test_cancel_deducts_credit(self, client, db, sample_users, imminent_reservation):
        """A late_cancel CreditHistory entry is recorded with -1 points."""
        _login(client)
        client.post(f"/booking/{imminent_reservation.id}/cancel")
        entry = CreditHistory.query.filter_by(
            user_id=sample_users["customer"].id,
            event_type="late_cancel",
        ).first()
        assert entry is not None
        assert entry.points == -1

    def test_cancel_response_warns_breach(self, client, sample_users, imminent_reservation):
        """Response body mentions the breach / 12-hour window."""
        _login(client)
        resp = client.post(f"/booking/{imminent_reservation.id}/cancel")
        assert b"12" in resp.data or b"breach" in resp.data.lower() or b"late" in resp.data.lower()


# ── Cancel outside 12 hours — no breach ───────────────────────────────────────

class TestCancelOutside12Hours:
    def test_cancel_returns_200(self, client, sample_users, db, sample_session):
        """POST /booking/<id>/cancel with >12 h remaining returns 200."""
        _login(client)
        result = create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, sample_session.id)
        resp = client.post(f"/booking/{result['reservation_id']}/cancel")
        assert resp.status_code == 200

    def test_no_breach_flag(self, client, db, sample_users, sample_session):
        """breach_flag stays False for a timely cancel."""
        _login(client)
        result = create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, sample_session.id)
        client.post(f"/booking/{result['reservation_id']}/cancel")
        res = Reservation.query.get(result["reservation_id"])
        assert res.breach_flag is not True


# ── Reschedule within 12 hours — allowed, breach recorded ─────────────────────

class TestRescheduleWithin12Hours:
    def test_reschedule_returns_200(self, client, db, sample_users,
                                    imminent_reservation, future_session):
        """POST /booking/<id>/reschedule within 12 h is allowed and returns 200."""
        _login(client)
        resp = client.post(
            f"/booking/{imminent_reservation.id}/reschedule",
            data={"new_session_id": str(future_session.id)},
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 200

    def test_reschedule_sets_breach_flag(self, client, db, sample_users,
                                         imminent_reservation, future_session):
        """Old reservation has breach_flag=True after a late reschedule."""
        _login(client)
        client.post(
            f"/booking/{imminent_reservation.id}/reschedule",
            data={"new_session_id": str(future_session.id)},
        )
        db.session.refresh(imminent_reservation)
        assert imminent_reservation.breach_flag is True

    def test_reschedule_deducts_credit(self, client, db, sample_users,
                                       imminent_reservation, future_session):
        """A late_cancel CreditHistory entry is recorded for a late reschedule."""
        _login(client)
        client.post(
            f"/booking/{imminent_reservation.id}/reschedule",
            data={"new_session_id": str(future_session.id)},
        )
        entry = CreditHistory.query.filter_by(
            user_id=sample_users["customer"].id,
            event_type="late_cancel",
        ).first()
        assert entry is not None
        assert entry.points == -1
"}

        resp = client.post(f"/booking/{result['reservation_id']}/cancel")
        assert resp.status_code == 200

    def test_no_breach_flag(self, client, db, sample_users, sample_session):
        """breach_flag stays False for a timely cancel."""
        _login(client)
        result = create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, sample_session.id)
        client.post(f"/booking/{result['reservation_id']}/cancel")
        res = Reservation.query.get(result["reservation_id"])
        assert res.breach_flag is not True


# ── Reschedule within 12 hours — allowed, breach recorded ─────────────────────

class TestRescheduleWithin12Hours:
    def test_reschedule_returns_200(self, client, db, sample_users,
                                    imminent_reservation, future_session):
        """POST /booking/<id>/reschedule within 12 h is allowed and returns 200."""
        _login(client)
        resp = client.post(
            f"/booking/{imminent_reservation.id}/reschedule",
            data={"new_session_id": str(future_session.id)},
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 200

    def test_reschedule_sets_breach_flag(self, client, db, sample_users,
                                         imminent_reservation, future_session):
        """Old reservation has breach_flag=True after a late reschedule."""
        _login(client)
        client.post(
            f"/booking/{imminent_reservation.id}/reschedule",
            data={"new_session_id": str(future_session.id)},
        )
        db.session.refresh(imminent_reservation)
        assert imminent_reservation.breach_flag is True

    def test_reschedule_deducts_credit(self, client, db, sample_users,
                                       imminent_reservation, future_session):
        """A late_cancel CreditHistory entry is recorded for a late reschedule."""
        _login(client)
        client.post(
            f"/booking/{imminent_reservation.id}/reschedule",
            data={"new_session_id": str(future_session.id)},
        )
        entry = CreditHistory.query.filter_by(
            user_id=sample_users["customer"].id,
            event_type="late_cancel",
        ).first()
        assert entry is not None
        assert entry.points == -1
"}

Booking funnel analytics are defined but not wired into the actual booking UI flow.
  - Brief rationale: Funnel computation depends on `view_schedule`, `booking_start`, `booking_complete` events, but event creation is only exposed via generic analytics endpoint and not triggered in booking pages/routes.
  - Evidence: Funnel relies on these events in `studioops/app/services/analytics_service.py:263`, `studioops/app/services/analytics_service.py:273`; no `track_event(...)` calls in booking logic (`track_event` only seen in `studioops/app/blueprints/analytics.py:41`, `studioops/app/blueprints/analytics.py:64`); schedule template has no analytics event emission (`studioops/app/templates/booking/schedule.html:1`).
  - Impact: Admin funnel metrics can remain zero/inaccurate during normal use, weakening measurable service-improvement goals.
  - Minimum actionable fix: Emit funnel events from booking interaction points (schedule render, booking initiation, successful booking) with server-side fallbacks to avoid client-only blind spots.

1	"""
2	Booking blueprint — all booking-related HTTP endpoints.
3	
4	URL layout (blueprint registered with no prefix so /schedule lives at root):
5	  GET  /schedule                                  – public schedule browser
6	  GET  /schedule/sessions/<id>                    – session detail
7	  GET  /booking/                                  – alias → my-bookings (backward compat)
8	  GET  /booking/my-bookings                       – user's booking dashboard
9	  GET  /booking/available-sessions                – HTMX: sessions for reschedule modal
10	  POST /booking/reserve                           – create reservation
11	  POST /booking/waitlist                          – join waitlist
12	  POST /booking/<reservation_id>/cancel           – cancel reservation
13	  POST /booking/<reservation_id>/reschedule       – reschedule reservation
14	  POST /booking/waitlist/<waitlist_id>/leave      – leave waitlist
15	
16	Business logic lives entirely in app/services/booking_service.py.
17	"""
18	import json
19	import logging
20	from datetime import datetime
21	
22	from flask import (
23	    Blueprint, render_template, request, redirect,
24	    url_for, flash, abort, make_response,
25	)
26	from flask_login import current_user
27	
28	from app.extensions import db
29	from app.models.studio import StudioSession, Reservation, Resource
30	from app.models.user import User
31	from app.services import booking_service
32	from app.utils.decorators import login_required, role_required
33	
34	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
35	
36	# No url_prefix — routes carry their full paths explicitly so /schedule lives
37	# at root while /booking/* routes live under /booking.
38	booking_bp = Blueprint("booking", __name__)
39	
40	
41	# ── helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
42	
43	def _is_htmx() -> bool:
44	    return bool(request.headers.get("HX-Request"))
45	
46	
47	def _to_iso(date_mm_dd_yyyy: str) -> str:
48	    """Convert MM/DD/YYYY → YYYY-MM-DD (ISO) for HTML date input values."""
49	    try:
50	        return datetime.strptime(date_mm_dd_yyyy, "%m/%d/%Y").strftime("%Y-%m-%d")

75	@booking_bp.route("/schedule")
76	@login_required
77	def schedule():
78	    """
79	    GET /schedule
80	
81	    Renders the class schedule for a given date.  Supports HTMX partial
82	    refresh: when the request carries an HX-Request header only the session
83	    list fragment is returned so the page does not reload on date navigation.
84	    """
85	    today = datetime.utcnow().strftime("%m/%d/%Y")
86	    raw_date = request.args.get("date", today)
87	
88	    # Accept both ISO (YYYY-MM-DD from HTML date input) and MM/DD/YYYY formats
89	    date_str = _normalise_date(raw_date) or today
90	
91	    instructor_id = request.args.get("instructor_id", type=int, default=None)
92	
93	    sessions = []
94	    try:
95	        sessions = booking_service.get_sessions_for_date(date_str, instructor_id)
96	    except ValueError as exc:
97	        logger.warning("schedule: bad date param '%s': %s", date_str, exc)
98	        flash(str(exc), "error")
99	        date_str = today
100	        sessions = booking_service.get_sessions_for_date(date_str)
101	
102	    # ISO version for the <input type="date"> value attribute
103	    selected_date_iso = _to_iso(date_str)
104	
105	    if _is_htmx():
106	        return render_template(
107	            "partials/booking/session_list.html",
108	            sessions=sessions,
109	            selected_date=date_str,
110	        )
111	
112	    instructors = User.query.filter_by(role="staff", is_active=True).order_by(
113	        User.username.asc()
114	    ).all()
115	
116	    return render_template(
117	        "booking/schedule.html",
118	        sessions=sessions,
119	        selected_date=date_str,
120	        selected_date_iso=selected_date_iso,
121	        instructors=instructors,
122	        selected_instructor_id=instructor_id,
123	    )
124	
125	
126	# ── Route 2 — session detail ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
127	
128	@booking_bp.route("/schedule/sessions/<int:session_id>")
129	@login_required
130	def session_detail(session_id: int):
131	    """
132	    GET /schedule/sessions/<session_id>
133	
134	    Full detail view for a single studio session.  Staff and admins also see
135	    the list of confirmed attendees.
136	    """
137	    session = StudioSession.query.get_or_404(session_id)
138	
139	    instructor = User.query.get(session.instructor_id) if session.instructor_id else None
140	    room = Resource.query.get(session.room_id) if session.room_id else None
141	
142	    # Deserialise equipment IDs and resolve to Resource objects
143	    try:
144	        equipment_ids = json.loads(session.equipment_ids or "[]")
145	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
146	        equipment_ids = []
147	    equipment = Resource.query.filter(Resource.id.in_(equipment_ids)).all() if equipment_ids else []
148	
149	    booked_count = Reservation.query.filter_by(
150	        session_id=session_id, status="confirmed"
151	    ).count()
152	
153	    # Check whether the current user already holds a reservation
154	    user_reservation = Reservation.query.filter_by(
155	        user_id=current_user.id, session_id=session_id, status="confirmed"
156	    ).first()
157	
158	    attendees = None
159	    if current_user.role in ("staff", "admin"):
160	        confirmed_reservations = Reservation.query.filter_by(
161	            session_id=session_id, status="confirmed"
162	        ).all()
163	        attendees = [r.user for r in confirmed_reservations if r.user]
164	
165	    logger.debug("session_detail: session_id=%d viewed by user=%d", session_id, current_user.id)
166	
167	    return render_template(
168	        "booking/session_detail.html",
169	        session=session,
170	        instructor=instructor,
171	        room=room,
172	        equipment=equipment,
173	        booked_count=booked_count,
174	        spots_remaining=max(0, session.capacity - booked_count),
175	        is_full=(booked_count >= session.capacity),
176	        user_reservation=user_reservation,
177	        attendees=attendees,
178	    )
179	
180	
181	# ── Route 3 — reserve ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
182	
183	@booking_bp.route("/booking/reserve", methods=["POST"])
184	@login_required
185	def reserve():
186	    """
187	    POST /booking/reserve

All 396 tests pass. Changes in `app/blueprints/booking.py`:

- **`_track()` helper** — thin wrapper around `analytics_service.track_event()` that passes the standard request context fields (user, session cookie, IP, user-agent) and swallows any exception so analytics never breaks a booking flow.

- **`schedule()`** — emits `view_schedule` on full-page loads (HTMX partial refreshes are skipped to avoid double-counting date navigations).

- **`session_detail()`** — emits `booking_start` when a customer views a session detail page (the natural point of booking intent; staff/admin views are excluded so staff roster checks don't inflate funnel numbers).

- **`reserve()`** — emits `booking_complete` only on `result["success"]`, carrying `session_id` and `reservation_id` for join-back queries.

Object-level authorization is incomplete for staff check-in/no-show actions.
  - Brief rationale: Any staff/admin can hit check-in/no-show endpoints for arbitrary reservation IDs; no instructor/session ownership check in endpoint or service.
  - Evidence: Endpoint directly forwards to service in `studioops/app/blueprints/staff.py:143`, `studioops/app/blueprints/staff.py:166`; services do not verify acting staff is assigned to reservation’s session in `studioops/app/services/staff_service.py:239`, `studioops/app/services/staff_service.py:329`.
  - Impact: Unauthorized operational updates across staff boundaries (improper completion/no-show marking).
  - Minimum actionable fix: Enforce per-session authorization (instructor-of-session or admin) before mutating reservation status.

Changes to `app/services/staff_service.py`:

Both `perform_checkin()` and `mark_no_show()` now include a **Step 1b** authorization check immediately after loading the reservation:
- Loads the acting user; returns an error if not found.
- If the acting user is **admin**: passes through (admins retain full operational access).
- If the acting user is **staff**: verifies `session.instructor_id == staff_id`; returns a 403-style service error if they don't own the session.

The check uses the `session_id` from the already-loaded reservation, so it adds one extra query per call (the `User` and `StudioSession` lookups are already performed later in both functions anyway).

  - Impact: Out-of-box operation can violate retention requirement and reduce restore safety window.
  - Minimum actionable fix: Set default CLI retention to 30 and align docs/automation examples accordingly.

def test_reschedule_within_12h_breach(app, db, sample_users, sample_room):
    """Reschedule <12 hours before start: allowed, breach flag set, credit deducted."""
    with app.app_context():
        start = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(hours=6)
        target_start = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=2)
        session1 = StudioSession(
            title="Soon Session",
            instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id,
            room_id=sample_room.id,
            start_time=start,
            end_time=start + timedelta(hours=1),
            capacity=15,
            is_active=True,
        )
        session2 = StudioSession(
            title="Later Session",
            instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id,
            room_id=sample_room.id,
            start_time=target_start,
            end_time=target_start + timedelta(hours=1),
            capacity=15,
            is_active=True,
        )
        db.session.add_all([session1, session2])
        db.session.commit()

        create_result = create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, session1.id)
        result = reschedule_reservation(
            create_result["reservation_id"], session2.id, sample_users["customer"].id
        )

        assert result["success"] is True
        assert result["breach"] is True

        old = Reservation.query.get(create_result["reservation_id"])
        assert old.breach_flag is True
        assert old.status == "rescheduled"

        from app.models.analytics import CreditHistory
        credit = CreditHistory.query.filter_by(
            user_id=sample_users["customer"].id, event_type="late_cancel"
        ).first()
        assert credit is not None
        assert credit.points == -1


def test_reschedule_creates_linked_reservation"}

def _make_started_session_with_reservation(db, instructor, customer, room):

        session_id=session.id,
        status="confirmed",
    )
    db.session.add(reservation)
    db.session.commit()
    return session, reservation


def _make_ended_session_with_reservation(db, instructor, customer, room):
    """Helper: session ended 10 min ago, with one confirmed reservation."""
    start = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(hours=2)
    end = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(minutes=10)
    session = StudioSession(
        title="Ended Session",
        instructor_id=instructor.id,
        room_id=room.id,
        start_time=start,
        end_time=end,
        capacity=15,
        is_active=True,
    )

        user_id=customer.id,
        session_id=session.id,
        status="confirmed",
    )
    db.session.add(reservation)
    db.session.commit()
    return session, reservation


def _make_other_staff(db):
    from app.services.auth_service import hash_password
    from app.models.user import User
    other = User(
        username="otherstaff",
        email="otherstaff@test.com",
        role="staff",
        credit_score=100,
        password_hash=hash_password("TestPass123!"),
    )
    db.session.add(other)
    db.session.commit()
    return other


def test_checkin_unauthorized_staff_rejected(app, db, sample_users, sample_room):
    """Staff B cannot check in a customer in Staff A's session."""
    with app.app_context():
        other_staff = _make_other_staff(db)
        _, reservation = _make_started_session_with_reservation(
            db, sample_users["staff"], sample_users["customer"], sample_room
        )
        result = staff_service.perform_checkin(reservation.id, other_staff.id)
        assert result["success"] is False
        assert "not authorized" in result["reason"].lower()


def test_checkin_authorized_instructor_succeeds(app, db, sample_users, sample_room):
    """The assigned instructor can check in customers in their own session."""
    with app.app_context():
        _, reservation = _make_started_session_with_reservation(
            db, sample_users["staff"], sample_users["customer"], sample_room
        )
        result = staff_service.perform_checkin(reservation.id, sample_users["staff"].id)
        assert result["success"] is True


def test_checkin_admin_can_act_on_any_session(app, db, sample_users, sample_room):
    """Admin can check in customers regardless of session instructor."""
    with app.app_context():
        _, reservation = _make_started_session_with_reservation(
            db, sample_users["staff"], sample_users["customer"], sample_room
        )
        result = staff_service.perform_checkin(reservation.id, sample_users["admin"].id)
        assert result["success"] is True


def test_noshow_unauthorized_staff_rejected(app, db, sample_users, sample_room):
    """Staff B cannot mark no-show in Staff A's session."""
    with app.app_context():
        other_staff = _make_other_staff(db)
        _, reservation = _make_ended_session_with_reservation(
            db, sample_users["staff"], sample_users["customer"], sample_room
        )
        result = staff_service.mark_no_show(reservation.id, other_staff.id)
        assert result["success"] is False
        assert "not authorized" in result["reason"].lower()


def test_noshow_authorized_instructor_succeeds(app, db, sample_users, sample_room):
    """The assigned instructor can mark no-shows in their own session."""
    with app.app_context():
        _, reservation = _make_ended_session_with_reservation(
            db, sample_users["staff"], sample_users["customer"], sample_room
        )
        result = staff_service.mark_no_show(reservation.id, sample_users["staff"].id)
        assert result["success"] is True


def test_noshow_admin_can_act_on_any_session(app, db, sample_users, sample_room):
    """Admin can mark no-shows regardless of session instructor."""
    with app.app_context():
        _, reservation = _make_ended_session_with_reservation(
            db, sample_users["staff"], sample_users["customer"], sample_room
        )
        result = staff_service.mark_no_show(reservation.id, sample_users["admin"].id)
        assert result["success"] is True


# ── Resource availability tests ───────────────────────────────────────────────

def test_instructor_conflict_detected"}

{"file_path": "/home/leul/Documents/task-7/repo/studioops/API_tests/test_booking_analytics.py", "content": """"
API-level tests: booking UI flow emits the three funnel analytics events.

view_schedule   — emitted on full (non-HTMX) GET /schedule
booking_start   — emitted on GET /schedule/sessions/<id> by a customer
booking_complete — emitted on successful POST /booking/reserve
"""
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

from app.models.analytics import AnalyticsEvent
from app.models.studio import StudioSession


def _login(client, username="testcustomer", password="TestPass123!"):
    client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        data={"identifier": username, "password": password},
        follow_redirects=True,
    )


def _event_count(db, event_type):
    return AnalyticsEvent.query.filter_by(event_type=event_type).count()


# ── view_schedule ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestViewScheduleEvent:
    def test_full_page_load_emits_event(self, client, db, sample_users, sample_session):
        """GET /schedule (full page) records a view_schedule event."""
        _login(client)
        before = _event_count(db, "view_schedule")
        client.get("/schedule")
        assert _event_count(db, "view_schedule") == before + 1

    def test_htmx_partial_does_not_emit_event(self, client, db, sample_users, sample_session):
        """GET /schedule with HX-Request (HTMX partial) must not double-count."""
        _login(client)
        before = _event_count(db, "view_schedule")
        client.get("/schedule", headers={"HX-Request": "true"})
        assert _event_count(db, "view_schedule") == before

    def test_event_page_field_set(self, client, db, sample_users, sample_session):
        """view_schedule event's page field is '/schedule'."""
        _login(client)
        client.get("/schedule")
        event = AnalyticsEvent.query.filter_by(event_type="view_schedule").first()
        assert event is not None
        assert event.page == "/schedule"


# ── booking_start ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestBookingStartEvent:
    def test_session_detail_emits_booking_start_for_customer(
        self, client, db, sample_users, sample_session
    ):
        """GET /schedule/sessions/<id> by a customer emits booking_start."""
        _login(client)
        before = _event_count(db, "booking_start")
        client.get(f"/schedule/sessions/{sample_session.id}")
        assert _event_count(db, "booking_start") == before + 1

    def test_session_detail_no_event_for_staff(
        self, client, db, sample_users, sample_session
    ):
        """Staff viewing a session detail page must NOT emit booking_start."""
        _login(client, "teststaff")
        before = _event_count(db, "booking_start")
        client.get(f"/schedule/sessions/{sample_session.id}")
        assert _event_count(db, "booking_start") == before

    def test_event_carries_session_id(self, client, db, sample_users, sample_session):
        """booking_start event data includes the session_id."""
        import json
        _login(client)
        client.get(f"/schedule/sessions/{sample_session.id}")
        event = AnalyticsEvent.query.filter_by(event_type="booking_start").first()
        assert event is not None
        data = json.loads(event.data or "{}")
        assert data.get("session_id") == sample_session.id


# ── booking_complete ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestBookingCompleteEvent:
    def test_successful_reserve_emits_event(
        self, client, db, sample_users, sample_session
    ):
        """POST /booking/reserve success records a booking_complete event."""
        _login(client)
        before = _event_count(db, "booking_complete")
        client.post("/booking/reserve", data={"session_id": str(sample_session.id)})
        assert _event_count(db, "booking_complete") == before + 1

    def test_failed_reserve_does_not_emit_event(
        self, client, db, sample_users, sample_session
    ):
        """A duplicate booking attempt must not emit booking_complete."""
        _login(client)
        client.post("/booking/reserve", data={"session_id": str(sample_session.id)})
        before = _event_count(db, "booking_complete")
        client.post("/booking/reserve", data={"session_id": str(sample_session.id)})
        assert _event_count(db, "booking_complete") == before

    def test_event_carries_reservation_id(
        self, client, db, sample_users, sample_session
    ):
        """booking_complete event data includes reservation_id."""
        import json
        _login(client)
        client.post("/booking/reserve", data={"session_id": str(sample_session.id)})
        event = AnalyticsEvent.query.filter_by(event_type="booking_complete").first()
        assert event is not None
        data = json.loads(event.data or "{}")
        assert "reservation_id" in data
        assert data.get("session_id") == sample_session.id


# ── Funnel coherence ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestFunnelCoherence:
    def test_full_flow_populates_all_three_stages(
        self, client, db, sample_users, sample_session
    ):
        """
        Simulate a complete customer journey and confirm all three funnel
        event types are recorded in the database.
        """
        _login(client)
        client.get("/schedule")                                          # view_schedule
        client.get(f"/schedule/sessions/{sample_session.id}")           # booking_start
        client.post("/booking/reserve",                                  # booking_complete
                    data={"session_id": str(sample_session.id)})

        assert _event_count(db, "view_schedule") >= 1
        assert _event_count(db, "booking_start") >= 1
        assert _event_count(db, "booking_complete") >= 1
"}

413 tests pass (396 → +17 new). Summary of what was added:

**`unit_tests/test_booking.py`** — `test_reschedule_within_12h_breach`: verifies that `reschedule_reservation()` within 12 hours returns `success=True`, `breach=True`, sets `breach_flag` on the old reservation, and records a `late_cancel` credit entry with `-1` points.

**`unit_tests/test_staff.py`** — 6 new tests covering the cross-staff authorization boundary:
- `test_checkin_unauthorized_staff_rejected` / `test_noshow_unauthorized_staff_rejected`: Staff B gets a failure with "not authorized" when acting on Staff A's session.
- `test_checkin_authorized_instructor_succeeds` / `test_noshow_authorized_instructor_succeeds`: The assigned instructor succeeds.
- `test_checkin_admin_can_act_on_any_session` / `test_noshow_admin_can_act_on_any_session`: Admin bypasses the instructor check.

**`API_tests/test_booking_analytics.py`** — 11 new tests across 4 classes:
- `TestViewScheduleEvent`: full-page load emits the event; HTMX partials do not; `page` field is correct.
- `TestBookingStartEvent`: customer session detail emits `booking_start`; staff view does not; `session_id` is in the event data.
- `TestBookingCompleteEvent`: successful reserve emits the event; failed duplicate does not; `reservation_id` and `session_id` are in data.
- `TestFunnelCoherence`: simulates the full customer journey and asserts all three funnel stages are populated.

1	"""
2	API-level tests for staff HTTP endpoints.
3	All interactions go through the Flask test client.
4	"""
5	import pytest
6	from datetime import datetime, timedelta
7	
8	from app.models.studio import Reservation, StudioSession, Resource
9	
10	
11	def test_staff_schedule_requires_staff_role(client, login_as, sample_users):
12	    """Customer cannot access /staff/schedule — returns 403."""
13	    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
14	    resp = client.get("/staff/schedule")
15	    assert resp.status_code == 403
16	
17	
18	def test_staff_schedule_returns_200(client, login_as, sample_users):
19	    """Staff can access their schedule."""
20	    login_as("teststaff", "TestPass123!")
21	    resp = client.get("/staff/schedule")
22	    assert resp.status_code == 200
23	
24	
25	def test_staff_schedule_htmx_returns_fragment(client, login_as, sample_users):
26	    """HTMX request to /staff/schedule returns a fragment (no DOCTYPE)."""
27	    login_as("teststaff", "TestPass123!")
28	    resp = client.get("/staff/schedule", headers={"HX-Request": "true"})
29	    assert resp.status_code == 200
30	    assert b"<!DOCTYPE" not in resp.data
31	
32	
33	def test_admin_can_access_staff_schedule(client, login_as, sample_users):
34	    """Admin role can also access the staff schedule."""
35	    login_as("testadmin", "TestPass123!")
36	    resp = client.get("/staff/schedule")
37	    assert resp.status_code == 200
38	
39	
40	def test_checkin_endpoint(client, login_as, sample_users, completed_session_with_reservation):
41	    """POST /staff/checkin/{id} returns 200 and updated row."""
42	    login_as("teststaff", "TestPass123!")
43	    data = completed_session_with_reservation
44	    resp = client.post(f"/staff/checkin/{data['reservation'].id}")
45	    assert resp.status_code == 200
46

49	    """POST /staff/no-show/{id} returns 200 and updated row."""
50	    login_as("teststaff", "TestPass123!")
51	    data = ended_session_with_reservation
52	    resp = client.post(f"/staff/no-show/{data['reservation'].id}")
53	    assert resp.status_code == 200
54	
55	
56	def test_customer_cannot_checkin(client, login_as, sample_users, completed_session_with_reservation):
57	    """Customer role gets 403 on check-in endpoint."""
58	    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
59	    data = completed_session_with_reservation

67	    data = ended_session_with_reservation
68	    resp = client.post(f"/staff/no-show/{data['reservation'].id}")
69	    assert resp.status_code == 403

72	def test_create_session_success(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_room, db):
73	    """POST /staff/sessions creates a new session and returns 201."""
74	    login_as("testadmin", "TestPass123!")
75	    tomorrow = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=1)
76	    resp = client.post("/staff/sessions", data={
77	        "title": "New Yoga",
78	        "description": "A new class",
79	        "instructor_id": str(sample_users["staff"].id),
80	        "room_id": str(sample_room.id),

82	        "start_time": "10:00",
83	        "end_time": "11:00",

85	    })

89	def test_create_session_requires_admin(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_room):
90	    """Staff (non-admin) cannot create sessions."""
91	    login_as("teststaff", "TestPass123!")

# ── Cross-staff object-level authorization (API-level) ────────────────────────

@pytest.fixture
def other_staff_user(db):
    """Second staff member (not the instructor on any pre-built session)."""
    from app.models.user import User
    from app.services.auth_service import hash_password
    user = User(
        username="otherstaffuser",
        email="otherstaff@test.com",
        role="staff",
        credit_score=100,
        password_hash=hash_password("TestPass123!"),
    )
    db.session.add(user)
    db.session.commit()
    return user


def test_checkin_by_non_owner_staff_rejected(
    client, login_as, sample_users, other_staff_user,
    completed_session_with_reservation, db,
):
    """POST /staff/checkin/<id>: staff who does not own the session gets 200 error fragment."""
    login_as("otherstaffuser", "TestPass123!")
    data = completed_session_with_reservation
    resp = client.post(f"/staff/checkin/{data['reservation'].id}")
    # Service returns success=False → blueprint returns error fragment (200 with error HTML)
    # or a non-201 status. The key check: reservation must NOT become completed.
    db.session.refresh(data["reservation"])
    assert data["reservation"].status != "completed"


def test_checkin_by_owner_staff_succeeds(
    client, login_as, sample_users,
    completed_session_with_reservation, db,
):
    """POST /staff/checkin/<id>: the session instructor can check in."""
    login_as("teststaff", "TestPass123!")
    data = completed_session_with_reservation
    resp = client.post(f"/staff/checkin/{data['reservation'].id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    db.session.refresh(data["reservation"])
    assert data["reservation"].status == "completed"


def test_checkin_by_admin_succeeds_for_any_session(
    client, login_as, sample_users,
    completed_session_with_reservation, db,
):
    """POST /staff/checkin/<id>: admin can check in on any session."""
    login_as("testadmin", "TestPass123!")
    data = completed_session_with_reservation
    resp = client.post(f"/staff/checkin/{data['reservation'].id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    db.session.refresh(data["reservation"])
    assert data["reservation"].status == "completed"

):

    login_as("otherstaffuser", "TestPass123!")
    data = ended_session_with_reservation
    resp = client.post(f"/staff/no-show/{data['reservation'].id}")
    db.session.refresh(data["reservation"])

421 tests pass (413 + 8 new). Summary:

**`API_tests/test_staff_api.py`** — 6 new tests for cross-staff object-level authorization at the HTTP layer:
- `test_checkin_by_non_owner_staff_rejected` / `test_noshow_by_non_owner_staff_rejected` — a second staff user (`otherstaffuser`) hits the checkin/no-show endpoints for a session they don't instruct; reservation status must not change.
- `test_checkin_by_owner_staff_succeeds` / `test_noshow_by_owner_staff_succeeds` — the assigned instructor succeeds (200, status updated).
- `test_checkin_by_admin_succeeds_for_any_session` / `test_noshow_by_admin_succeeds_for_any_session` — admin bypasses instructor ownership.

**`API_tests/test_ops_api.py`** — 2 new tests for backup retention consistency:
- `test_backup_ui_default_retention_is_30` — asserts `value="30"` appears in the admin backups page HTML.
- `test_retention_endpoint_defaults_to_30` — POSTs to enforce-retention without `max_backups` and confirms it completes successfully with the 30-backup default.
